chief_complaint: A 34-year-old White woman presented with massive rectal bleeding 15 days after suction RBL of internal hemorrhoids.
history: The patient underwent suction RBL of internal hemorrhoids 15 days prior to presentation. She was not taking anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or NSAIDs and had no history of bleeding disorders.
exam: Flexible sigmoidoscopy revealed a 10-mm ulcer with a visible vessel at the previous banding site.
correct_diagnosis: Delayed bleeding after rubber band ligation of internal hemorrhoids
differentials: Delayed bleeding after rubber band ligation of internal hemorrhoids
Post-procedure bleeding due to anticoagulant use
Post-procedure bleeding due to antiplatelet use
Post-procedure bleeding due to NSAID use
correct_ranking: Delayed bleeding after rubber band ligation of internal hemorrhoids
Post-procedure bleeding due to anticoagulant use
Post-procedure bleeding due to antiplatelet use
Post-procedure bleeding due to NSAID use
